title: Palindrome Partitioning
description: Given a string s, partition s such that every substring of the partition is a palindrome. Return all possible palindrome partitioning of s.

examples.explanation: The string "aab" can be partitioned into two valid sets: ['a', 'a', 'b'] and ['aa', 'b']. Each substring is a palindrome.
The string "a" itself is a single character and is a palindrome, thus it can only be partitioned as [['a']].
The string "racecar" can be partitioned into multiple valid sets including individual letters, the substring "aceca", and the whole string itself since they are all palindromes.

constraints: The input string s has a length of at most 16 characters.
All characters in s consist of lowercase English letters.
hints: Consider using backtracking to explore all possible partitions.
You can check if a substring is a palindrome by comparing it to its reverse.
Keep track of the current partition and backtrack when necessary.
Use memoization to improve efficiency when checking for palindromes.
